questions.text: Which CV format is the safest and most expected default for most job seekers?
options: Functional (skills-first layout)
Reverse-chronological (newest jobs first)
Graphic layout (with icons and visual timelines)
Combination (skills-focused header with chronological experience)
questions.explanation: Reverse-chronological is the safest default because it is easily read by all ATS parsers and is preferred by recruiters who want to see your career progression quickly.

questions.text: What design element is most likely to scramble or break an Applicant Tracking System (ATS)?
options: Standard fonts like Arial or Georgia
Single-column vertical layouts
PDF file exports
Multi-column sidebars, tables, and text boxes
questions.explanation: Multi-column structures, text boxes, and tables are often parsed left-to-right straight across, scrambling unrelated columns of text together.

questions.text: Which of these is the most effective structure for an experience bullet point?
options: Responsible for handling customer inquiries and administrative duties.
A detailed paragraph explaining your daily schedule and routines.
Action Verb + Actions Taken + Quantifiable / Measurable Result.
A list of software programs you used during your time at the job.
questions.explanation: Quantified bullets (e.g., 'Grew sales 25% by implementing X') demonstrate concrete impact and catch the eye far better than boring descriptions of responsibilities.

questions.text: When applying for jobs in the US, UK, or Canada, should you include a photo on your resume?
options: Yes, it helps recruiters put a face to your name.
No, it triggers automated bias filters and legal compliance rejections.
Only if you use a high-quality professional headshot.
Only in black and white to keep file sizes low.
questions.explanation: In these countries, companies frequently auto-reject resumes with photos to protect themselves from accusations of discrimination based on age, race, or gender.

questions.text: What is the best way to handle keywords from the job description on your CV?
options: Copy and paste the entire job posting in tiny white text at the bottom.
Repeat the job title 10-15 times in your experience bullets.
Avoid using them so your CV doesn't look copied.
Mirror exact terms and acronyms naturally within your experience and skills sections.
questions.explanation: Mirroring exact keywords and spelling is crucial for ATS matching, but it must read naturally to the human recruiter. White text tricks or stuffing get flagged as spam.
